channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Trolley, 12th June
item.description:
Trolley dumped by bus stop

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Flaxton Road, Plumstead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Overgrown bushes, 12th June
item.description:
The bushes at one end ,near the lampost are almost half way across the pavement.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Nightingale Road, Poverest, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping Westdale Road's grass bank, 12th June
item.description:
A broken down wardrobe and rubbish that looks like a house clearance have been dumped on the grass bank opposite 4 Westdale Road. I reported this fly tipping a week ago on the 4th Jun'26. I've just seen the council's response which is "This is caretaker’s responsibility". Westdale Road and its grass bank are NOT part of any council estate. They are NOT managed by a caretaker. Fly tipping and street cleaning are covered by council staff who look after the rest of the borough, excluding council estates.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Westdale Road, Woolwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Area Needs Cleaning and Tidying, 12th June
item.description:
I am writing to raise concerns about the condition of the greenspace outside the houses, which is maintained by the council. The area has deteriorated significantly. It is littered with general waste as well as dead plants and foliage. The privet has died, leaving large gaps that have become overgrown with weeds. These weeds are now unsightly, spreading rapidly, and beginning to obstruct access to the shared steps. In addition, the street itself is affected by weeds growing through the pavement and is also accumulating rubbish. Overall, the area is in a poor state and requires a thorough clean-up and maintenance overhaul to restore it to a safe and tidy condition for residents. I would appreciate it if the council could arrange for this to be addressed as soon as possible.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Southover, Sundridge, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.description:
2 pallets plus another in the nearby footpath.

item.title: 2 man pro action tent & bits need removing pls, 12th June
item.description:

item.category: Street cleaning - Dead animal on street

item.title: Extensive growth of horsetail weed, 12th June
item.description:
From about halfway down West Hallowes to the bottom of the road on the left hand side going down, the pavements, gutters and edge of front walls have horsetail weed growing through, it’s extremely invasive and difficult to remove. Please spray as soon as possible, it is worse than last year and is creeping up the road

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): West Hallowes, Eltham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.description:

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): York Hill, West Norwood, Greater London

item.title: Tree is too big have roots damageing my wall and roots comeing up in my garden..., 12th June
item.description:
Tree roots comeing uup in garden and dameging the foundation of my property...

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Stembridge Road, Anerley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Nettles overhanging and causing danger to children including vulnerable children with mobility difficulities, 12th June
item.description:
The stinging nettles along this path have become incredibly overgrown and are encroaching significantly onto the walkway. Unfortunately, this morning a parent reported that their child fell directly into them and was badly stung. As you can imagine, this is a heavily used route for our pupils and families. Please see the attached photograph for reference, which illustrates the extent of the overgrowth. I understand that at least one parent has already logged this issue via your reporting portal, but given the injury to a child, the situation is now urgent. Please could arrangements be made for these nettles to be cleared over the weekend? It is vital that this path is made safe before the children return to school on Monday morning. Thank you for your prompt attention to this matter. I look forward to your confirmation that someone will be out to clear the path before Monday.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Riverside Road, North Cray, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Flat tyre, open windows. Abandoned, 12th June
item.description:
Flat tyre, open windows. Abandoned. 8 months
